title: Contributing to spant development
text: Contributions spant welcome generally take form bug reports/feature requests, documentation suggestions code changes via pull request.

title: Contributing to spant development
text: Please report bugs make feature requests via https://github.com/martin3141/spant/issues/. filing issue, important thing include minimal reproducible example can quickly verify problem, figure fix .

title: Contributing to spant development
text: like help new users particular aspect MRS data processing analysis please consider contributing short document R markdown format added package. examples please see doc folder main GitHub repository. Function reference documentation derived roxygen2 code comments best contributed via pull request, described .

title: Contributing to spant development
text: contribute change spant, follow steps: Create branch git make changes. Push branch github issue pull request (PR). Discuss pull request. Iterate either accept PR decide ’s good fit spant. ’re familiar git github, please start reading http://r-pkgs..co.nz/git.html. Please use following style http://adv-r..co.nz/Style.html. Please ensure checks pass devtools::check(args = c('---cran')) consider adding one unit tests repository tests directory confirm expected behavior devtools::test(). CI checks set run following pushes master branch using GitHub Actions, see https://orchid00.github.io/actions_sandbox details.

title: ABfit baseline options
text: good baseline estimate prerequisite accurate metabolite quantitation. default MRS analysis algorithm spant (ABfit) designed find accurate baseline estimates automatically adapting level baseline flexibility match data complexity. process inevitably balancing act baseline smooth (resulting greater bias), flexible (resulting greater variance). Therefore, ABfit number fitting options adjust baseline according user preference. vignette common adjustments demonstrated.

title: ABfit baseline options
text: Load spant analysis package: Read example dataset file simulate matching basis set: Run default ABfit analysis plot result:  fit looks good, smooth baseline significant signals residual (top trace) noise level. can find automatically determined level baseline smoothness inspecting results table fit_res object: baseline flexibility found 2 ED per ppm, ED effective dimension – analogous number spline functions required per ppm. Whilst automated fit looks reasonable first glance, let’s try convince can’t better manual adjustments algorithm.
code: library(spant) fname    <- system.file("extdata", "philips_spar_sdat_WS.SDAT", package = "spant") mrs_data <- read_mrs(fname, format = "spar_sdat") basis    <- sim_basis_1h_brain_press(mrs_data) fit_res <- fit_mrs(mrs_data, basis) plot(fit_res) fit_res$res_tab$bl_ed_pppm #> [1] 1.969325

title: ABfit baseline options
text: Changing default behaviour ABfit achieved supplying options structure fit_mrs function. abfit_opts function generates default fitting options, may modified supplying arguments. manually specify baseline flexibility set auto_bl_flex option FALSE set bl_ed_pppm option desired level. greater value results baseline flexibility, let’s try value 8 ED ppm:  baseline clearly flexible, resulting slightly improved residual, however baseline features likely due instability noise, rather true spectral features. next analysis let’s investigate 1 ED pppm:  Now much smoother (almost linear) baseline, comes cost broad unmodelled signals residual – ultimately resulting biased metabolite levels. alternative manually specifying fixed level baseline flexibility adjust criterion used automated estimation. aic_smoothing_factor can set smaller value (default = 5) encourage flexible baselines, whilst still adaptive broad spectral features:  can informative visualise individual spline components used baseline modelling saving results object:  default number spline functions ABfit 15 per PPM may verified plot. Let’s try increasing 25:  Clearly density spline functions increased, however baseline smoothness remains close default. general principle ABfit (derived P-splines) specify number baseline modelling spline functions, rely penalty factor encourage smoothness. Inspecting automatically determined level baseline flexibility shows ED per ppm value remains close default analysis despite change spline function density – precisely desired behaviour.
code: opts    <- abfit_opts(auto_bl_flex = FALSE, bl_ed_pppm = 8) fit_res <- fit_mrs(mrs_data, basis, opts = opts) plot(fit_res) opts    <- abfit_opts(auto_bl_flex = FALSE, bl_ed_pppm = 1) fit_res <- fit_mrs(mrs_data, basis, opts = opts) plot(fit_res) opts    <- abfit_opts(aic_smoothing_factor = 1) fit_res <- fit_mrs(mrs_data, basis, opts = opts) plot(fit_res) opts    <- abfit_opts(export_sp_fit = TRUE) fit_res <- fit_mrs(mrs_data, basis, opts = opts) stackplot(fit_res, omit_signals = basis$names) opts    <- abfit_opts(export_sp_fit = TRUE, bl_comps_pppm = 25) fit_res <- fit_mrs(mrs_data, basis, opts = opts) stackplot(fit_res, omit_signals = basis$names) fit_res$res_tab$bl_ed_pppm #> [1] 2.276357

title: Basis simulation
text: Basis simulation necessary step modern MRS analysis vignette explain achieve spant. advisable follow examples given metabolite simulation vignette following guide. Load spant package: basis set collection signals fit MRS data. spant start list molecular definitions containing relevant information signal - chemical shifts j-coupling values: next step convert chemical properties collection signals (spant basis_set object) sim_basis function. fitting, signal parameters (e.g. sampling frequency) pulse sequence (e.g. echo-time) must match MRS data acquisition protocol.  1H MRS broad resonances lipids macromolecules often included addition metabolites:  Note field strength often required simulate broad resonances linewidth usually specified ppm. spant also includes functions sim_basis_1h_brain sim_basis_1h_brain_press produce commonly used sets basis signals:  Basis sets can exported use LCModel write_basis function, sim_basis_1h_brain option lcm_compat remove signals usually generated within LCModel package:
code: library(spant) mol_list <- list(get_mol_paras("lac"),                  get_mol_paras("naa"),                  get_mol_paras("cr"),                  get_mol_paras("gpc")) basis <- sim_basis(mol_list, pul_seq = seq_slaser_ideal,                    acq_paras = def_acq_paras(N = 2048, fs = 2000, ft = 127.8e6),                    TE1 = 0.008, TE2 = 0.011, TE3 = 0.009)  stackplot(basis, xlim = c(4, 0.5), y_offset = 50, labels = basis$names) mol_list_mm <- append(mol_list, list(get_mol_paras("MM09", ft = 127.8e6)))  basis_mm <- sim_basis(mol_list_mm, pul_seq = seq_slaser_ideal,                    acq_paras = def_acq_paras(N = 2048, fs = 2000, ft = 127.8e6),                    TE1 = 0.008, TE2 = 0.011, TE3 = 0.009)  stackplot(basis_mm, xlim = c(4, 0.5), y_offset = 50, labels = basis_mm$names) basis <- sim_basis_1h_brain() stackplot(basis, xlim = c(4, 0.5), y_offset = 20, labels = basis$names) lcm_basis <- sim_basis_1h_brain() stackplot(lcm_basis, xlim = c(4, 0.5), y_offset = 20, labels = basis$names)

title: Introduction to spant
text: sophisticated approach scaling metabolite values involves use separate water-reference acquisition - can imported standard way: following code assumes voxel contains 100% white matter tissue scales metabolite values molal (mM) units (mol / kg tissue water) based method described Gasparovic et al MRM 2006 55(6):1219-26: alternative method scales metabolite values molar (mM) units (mol / Litre tissue) based assumptions outlined LCModel manual references therein (section 10.2). approach may preferred comparing results obtained LCModel TARQUIN. Note, “absolute” units attractive, large number assumptions metabolite water relaxation rates necessary arrive mM estimates. ’re confident able justify assumptions, scaling metabolite reference (eg tCr ) going better option cases. Simple metabolite referenced ratios also benefit reproducible due simplicity approach.
code: fname_wref <- system.file("extdata", "philips_spar_sdat_W.SDAT", package = "spant") mrs_data_wref <- read_mrs(fname_wref) p_vols <- c(WM = 100, GM = 0, CSF = 0) TE = 0.03 TR = 2 fit_res_molal <- scale_amp_molal_pvc(fit_res, mrs_data_wref, p_vols, TE, TR) fit_res_molal$res_tab$tNAA #> [1] 13.91832 fit_res_molar <- scale_amp_molar(fit_res, mrs_data_wref) #> Warning in scale_amp_molar(fit_res, mrs_data_wref): Function name #> (scale_amp_molar) is missleading and has been replaced with scale_amp_legacy. fit_res_molar$res_tab$tNAA #> [1] 6.818358

title: Metabolite simulation
text: Load spant package: Output list pre-defined molecules available simulation: Get print spin system myo-inositol: Simulate plot simulation 7 Tesla pulse acquire sequence (seq_pulse_acquire), apply 2 Hz line-broadening plot.  pulse sequences may simulated including: seq_cpmg_ideal, seq_mega_press_ideal, seq_press_ideal, seq_slaser_ideal, seq_spin_echo_ideal, seq_steam_ideal. Note sequences assume chemical shift displacement negligible. Next simulate 30 ms spin-echo sequence plot:  Finally simulate range echo-times plot results together see phase evolution:  See basis simulation vignette combine simulations basis set MRS analysis.
code: library(spant) get_mol_names() #>  [1] "2hg"       "a_glc"     "ace"       "ala"       "asc"       "asp"       #>  [7] "atp_31p"   "b_glc"     "bhb"       "cho"       "cho_rt"    "cit"       #> [13] "cr_ch2_rt" "cr_ch3_rt" "cr"        "gaba_jn"   "gaba"      "gaba_rt"   #> [19] "glc"       "gln"       "glu"       "glu_rt"    "gly"       "glyc"      #> [25] "gpc_31p"   "gpc"       "gpe_31p"   "gsh"       "h2o"       "ins"       #> [31] "ins_rt"    "lac"       "lac_rt"    "lip09"     "lip13a"    "lip13b"    #> [37] "lip20"     "lys"       "m_cr_ch2"  "mm_3t"     "mm09"      "mm12"      #> [43] "mm14"      "mm17"      "mm20"      "msm"       "naa"       "naa_rt"    #> [49] "naa2"      "naag_ch3"  "naag"      "nadh_31p"  "nadp_31p"  "pch_31p"   #> [55] "pch"       "pcr_31p"   "pcr"       "pe_31p"    "peth"      "pi_31p"    #> [61] "pyr"       "ser"       "sins"      "suc"       "tau"       "thr"       #> [67] "val" ins <- get_mol_paras("ins") print(ins) #> Name        : Ins #> Full name   : myo-Inositol #> Spin groups : 1 #> Source      : Proton NMR chemical shifts and coupling constants for brain metabolites. NMR Biomed. 2000; 13:129-153. #>  #> Spin group 1 #> ------------ #> Scaling factor : 1 #> Linewidth (Hz) : 0.5 #> L/G lineshape  : 0 #>  #>   nucleus chem_shift #> 1      1H     3.5217 #> 2      1H     4.0538 #> 3      1H     3.5217 #> 4      1H     3.6144 #> 5      1H     3.2690 #> 6      1H     3.6144 #>  #> j-coupling matrix #>        3.5217 4.0538 3.5217 3.6144 3.269 3.6144 #> 3.5217      -      -      -      -     -      - #> 4.0538  2.889      -      -      -     -      - #> 3.5217      -  3.006      -      -     -      - #> 3.6144      -      -  9.997      -     -      - #> 3.269       -      -      -  9.485     -      - #> 3.6144  9.998      -      -      - 9.482      - sim_mol(ins, ft = 300e6, N = 4096) |> lb(2) |> plot(xlim = c(3.8, 3.1)) ins_sim <- sim_mol(ins, seq_spin_echo_ideal, ft = 300e6, N = 4086, TE = 0.03) ins_sim |> lb(2) |> plot(xlim = c(3.8, 3.1)) sim_fn <- function(TE) {   te_sim <- sim_mol(ins, seq_spin_echo_ideal, ft = 300e6, N = 4086, TE = TE)   lb(te_sim, 2) }  te_vals <- seq(0, 2, 0.4)  lapply(te_vals, sim_fn) |> stackplot(y_offset = 150, xlim = c(3.8, 3.1),                                      labels = paste(te_vals * 100, "ms"))

title: Metabolite simulation
text: simple signals require j-coupling evolution, example singlets approximations macromolecule lipid resonances, get_uncoupled_mol function may used. example simulated two broad Gaussian resonances 1.3 1.4 ppm differing amplitudes:  Molecules aren’t defined within spant, need adjusting match particular scan, may manually defined constructing mol_parameters object. following code define imaginary molecule based Lactate, addition second spin group containing singlet 2.5 ppm. Whilst molecule defined single group, computationally efficient split non j-coupled spin systems way. Note lineshape set Lorentzian (Lorentz-Gauss factor lg = 0) width 2 Hz. generally good idea simulate resonances narrower lineshapes expect see experimental data, far easier make resonance broader narrower. next step output molecule definition formatted text plot .  happy new molecule correct, please consider contributing package think others benefit.
code: get_uncoupled_mol("Lip13", c(1.3, 1.4), c("1H", "1H"), c(2, 1), c(10, 10),                   c(1, 1)) |> sim_mol() |> plot(xlim = c(2, 0.8)) nucleus_a <- rep("1H", 4)  chem_shift_a <- c(4.0974, 1.3142, 1.3142, 1.3142)  j_coupling_mat_a <- matrix(0, 4, 4) j_coupling_mat_a[2,1] <- 6.933 j_coupling_mat_a[3,1] <- 6.933 j_coupling_mat_a[4,1] <- 6.933  spin_group_a <- list(nucleus = nucleus_a, chem_shift = chem_shift_a,                       j_coupling_mat = j_coupling_mat_a, scale_factor = 1,                      lw = 2, lg = 0)  nucleus_b <- c("1H") chem_shift_b <- c(2.5) j_coupling_mat_b <- matrix(0, 1, 1)  spin_group_b <- list(nucleus = nucleus_b, chem_shift = chem_shift_b,                       j_coupling_mat = j_coupling_mat_b, scale_factor = 3,                      lw = 2, lg = 0)  source <- "This text should include a reference on the origin of the chemical shift and j-coupling values."  custom_mol <- list(spin_groups = list(spin_group_a, spin_group_b), name = "Cus",               source = source, full_name = "Custom molecule")  class(custom_mol) <- "mol_parameters" print(custom_mol) #> Name        : Cus #> Full name   : Custom molecule #> Spin groups : 2 #> Source      : This text should include a reference on the origin of the chemical shift and j-coupling values. #>  #> Spin group 1 #> ------------ #> Scaling factor : 1 #> Linewidth (Hz) : 2 #> L/G lineshape  : 0 #>  #>   nucleus chem_shift #> 1      1H     4.0974 #> 2      1H     1.3142 #> 3      1H     1.3142 #> 4      1H     1.3142 #>  #> j-coupling matrix #>        4.0974 1.3142 1.3142 1.3142 #> 4.0974      -      -      -      - #> 1.3142  6.933      -      -      - #> 1.3142  6.933      -      -      - #> 1.3142  6.933      -      -      - #>  #> Spin group 2 #> ------------ #> Scaling factor : 3 #> Linewidth (Hz) : 2 #> L/G lineshape  : 0 #>  #>   nucleus chem_shift #> 1      1H        2.5 custom_mol |> sim_mol() |> lb(2) |> zf() |> plot(xlim = c(4.4, 0.5))

title: Common preprocessing steps
text: Load spant package: Load example data preprocessing: Plot spectral region 4 0.5 ppm:  Apply 180 degree phase adjustment plot:  Apply 3 Hz Guassian line broadening:  Zero fill data twice original length plot:  Apply HSVD filter residual water region plot together original data:  Apply 0.1 ppm frequency shift plot together original data:  Multiple processing commands may conveniently combined pipe operator “|>” :
code: library(spant) fname <- system.file("extdata", "philips_spar_sdat_WS.SDAT", package = "spant") mrs_data <- read_mrs(fname, format = "spar_sdat") plot(mrs_data, xlim = c(4, 0.5)) mrs_data_p180 <- phase(mrs_data, 180) plot(mrs_data_p180, xlim = c(4, 0.5)) mrs_data_lb <- lb(mrs_data, 3) plot(mrs_data_lb, xlim = c(4, 0.5)) mrs_data_zf <- zf(mrs_data, 2) plot(mrs_data_zf, xlim = c(4, 0.5)) mrs_data_filt <- hsvd_filt(mrs_data) stackplot(list(mrs_data, mrs_data_filt), xlim = c(5, 0.5), y_offset = 10,           col = c("black", "red"), labels = c("original", "filtered")) mrs_data_shift <- shift(mrs_data, 0.1, "ppm") stackplot(list(mrs_data, mrs_data_shift), xlim = c(4, 0.5), y_offset = 10,           col = c("black", "red"), labels = c("original", "shifted")) mrs_data_proc <- mrs_data |> hsvd_filt() |> lb(2) |> zf() plot(mrs_data_proc, xlim = c(5, 0.5))

title: MR Spectroscopy Analysis Tools
text: spant provides full suite tools build automated analysis pipelines Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy (MRS) data. following features algorithms included: Advanced fully-automated metabolite fitting algorithm - ABfit https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/mrm.28385, https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/mrm.30462. Robust retrospective frequency phase correction - RATS https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1002/mrm.27605. Support common MRS data formats including read / write NIfTI MRS - https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/mrm.29418. Flexible data types support single voxel, dynamic spectroscopic imaging. Raw data import individual coils dynamic measurements, eg support importing individual FIDs Siemens TWIX formatted data. Publication quality plotting. Extensive set pre-processing steps (phasing, coil-combination, zero-filling, HSVD filtering…). Quantum mechanical based simulation experimental design basis-set generation. Set metabolite, macromolecule lipid parameters typical brain analyses. Voxel registration anatomical images partial volume concentration corrections.

title: MR Spectroscopy Analysis Tools
text: online spant user manual (pdf version) written users minimal experience R MRS processing best place start. advanced users, help developing custom analysis pipeline can found . Introduction : https://martin3141.github.io/spant/articles/spant-intro.html Short tutorials : https://martin3141.github.io/spant/articles/ Function reference : https://martin3141.github.io/spant/reference/ spant library loaded library(spant), type ?spant console instructions access offline documentation. Note offline help available functions can quickly shown RStudio using ?function_name, eg ?read_mrs.

title: MR Spectroscopy Analysis Tools
text: Download install latest version R (https://cloud.r-project.org/), package manager using recent Linux distribution, eg sudo apt install r-base. also strongly recommended install RStudio Desktop (https://rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download) provide modern environment interactive data analysis. R RStudio installed, open RStudio application type following Console (lower left panel) install latest stable version spant: development version GitHub (requires devtools package):
code: install.packages("spant", dependencies = TRUE) install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("martin3141/spant", ref = "devel", dependencies = TRUE)

code: abfit_opts(   init_damping = 5,   maxiters = 1024,   max_shift_pre = 0.078,   max_shift_fine = NULL,   max_damping = 15,   max_phase = 360,   lambda = NULL,   ppm_left = 4,   ppm_right = 0.2,   zp = TRUE,   bl_ed_pppm = 2,   auto_bl_flex = TRUE,   bl_comps_pppm = 15,   adaptive_bl_comps_pppm = FALSE,   export_sp_fit = FALSE,   max_asym = 0.25,   max_basis_shift = 0.0078,   max_basis_damping = 2,   maxiters_pre = 1000,   algo_pre = "NLOPT_LN_NELDERMEAD",   min_bl_ed_pppm = NULL,   max_bl_ed_pppm = 7,   auto_bl_flex_n = 20,   pre_fit_bl_ed_pppm = 1,   remove_lip_mm_prefit = FALSE,   pre_align = TRUE,   max_pre_align_shift = 0.1,   pre_align_ref_freqs = c(2.01, 3.03, 3.22),   noise_region = c(-0.5, -2.5),   optimal_smooth_criterion = "maic",   aic_smoothing_factor = 5,   anal_jac = TRUE,   pre_fit_ppm_left = 4,   pre_fit_ppm_right = 1.8,   phi1_optim = FALSE,   phi1_init = 0,   max_dphi1 = 0.2,   max_basis_shift_broad = NULL,   max_basis_damping_broad = NULL,   ahat_calc_method = "lh_pnnls",   prefit_phase_search = TRUE,   freq_reg = NULL,   freq_reg_naa = NULL,   lb_reg = NULL,   asym_reg = NULL,   output_all_paras = FALSE,   output_all_paras_raw = FALSE,   input_paras_raw = NULL,   optim_lw_only = FALSE,   optim_lw_only_limit = 20,   lb_init = 0.001,   lb_init_approx_fit = FALSE,   zf_offset = NULL,   broad_asym = TRUE,   lb_reg_broad = NULL,   broad_glb = TRUE )

title: Return a list of options for an ABfit analysis. — abfit_opts
text: init_damping initial value Gaussian global damping parameter (Hz). poorly shimmed high field data may benefit larger value. maxiters maximum number iterations run detailed fit. max_shift_pre maximum allowable global shift applied approximate (pre-fit) phases analysis (ppm). max_shift_fine maximum allowable global shift applied detailed fit phase analysis (ppm). max_damping maximum permitted value global damping parameter (Hz). max_phase maximum absolute permitted value global zero-order phase term (degrees). Note, prefit_phase_search option constrained term. lambda manually set baseline smoothness parameter. ppm_left downfield frequency limit fitting range (ppm). ppm_right upfield frequency limit fitting range (ppm). zp zero pad data twice original length fitting. bl_ed_pppm manually set baseline smoothness parameter (ED per ppm). auto_bl_flex automatically determine level baseline smoothness. bl_comps_pppm spline basis density (signals per ppm). adaptive_bl_comps_pppm adjust spline basis density detailed fit phase, based required level smoothness, reduce computation time. export_sp_fit add fitted spline functions fit result. max_asym maximum allowable value asymmetry parameter. max_basis_shift maximum allowable frequency shift individual basis signals (ppm). max_basis_damping maximum allowable Lorentzian damping factor individual basis signals (Hz). maxiters_pre maximum iterations coarse (pre-)fit. algo_pre optimisation method coarse (pre-)fit. min_bl_ed_pppm minimum value candidate baseline flexibility analyses (ED per ppm). max_bl_ed_pppm minimum value candidate baseline flexibility analyses (ED per ppm). auto_bl_flex_n number candidate baseline analyses perform. pre_fit_bl_ed_pppm level baseline flexibility use coarse fitting stage algorithm (ED per ppm). remove_lip_mm_prefit remove broad signals coarse fitting stage algorithm. pre_align perform pre-alignment step coarse fitting. max_pre_align_shift maximum allowable shift pre-alignment step (ppm). pre_align_ref_freqs vector prominent spectral frequencies used pre-alignment step (ppm). noise_region spectral region estimate noise level (ppm). optimal_smooth_criterion method determine optimal smoothness. aic_smoothing_factor modification factor AIC calculation. Larger values result less flexible baselines. anal_jac use analytical approximation jacobian detailed fitting stage. pre_fit_ppm_left downfield frequency limit fitting range coarse fitting stage algorithm (ppm). pre_fit_ppm_right upfield frequency limit fitting range coarse fitting stage algorithm (ppm). phi1_optim apply optimise frequency dependant phase term. phi1_init initial value frequency dependant phase term (ms). max_dphi1 maximum allowable change initial frequency dependant phase term (ms). max_basis_shift_broad maximum allowable shift broad signals basis (ppm). Determined based name beginning Lip MM. default value set max_basis_shift. max_basis_damping_broad maximum allowable Lorentzian damping broad signals basis (Hz). Determined based name beginning Lip MM. default value set max_basis_damping. ahat_calc_method method calculate metabolite amplitudes. May one : "lh_pnnls" "ls". prefit_phase_search perform 1D search optimal phase prefit stage algorithm. freq_reg frequency shift parameter. freq_reg_naa frequency shift parameter NAA NAAG. lb_reg individual line broadening parameter. asym_reg lineshape asymmetry parameter. output_all_paras include fitting parameters fit table, e.g. individual shift damping factors basis set element. output_all_paras_raw include raw fitting parameters fit table. advanced diagnostic use . input_paras_raw input raw fitting parameters. advanced diagnostic use . optim_lw_only optimize global line-broadening term . optim_lw_only_limit limits line-breading term percentage starting value optim_lw_only TRUE. lb_init initial Lorentzian line broadening value (Hz) individual basis signals. Setting 0 clash minimum allowable value (eg hard constraint) detailed fit. lb_init_approx_fit apply lb_init basis approximate iterative fit. zf_offset offset number data points end FID zero-fill. Default NULL automatically set 50 points FID distortion flag set mrs_data. broad_asym apply asymmetric lineshape parameter broad signals names starting Lip MM. lb_reg_broad individual line broadening parameter broad (Lip / MM) signals. NULL defaults lb_reg. broad_glb apply global linewidth parameter broad signals names starting Lip MM.

title: Standard SVS 1H brain analysis pipeline. — fit_svs
code: fit_svs(   input,   w_ref = NULL,   output_dir = NULL,   mri = NULL,   mri_seg = NULL,   deface = FALSE,   segment_t1 = FALSE,   segment_t1_method = "rpyants",   external_basis = NULL,   append_external_basis = FALSE,   p_vols = NULL,   format = NULL,   pul_seq = NULL,   TE = NULL,   TR = NULL,   TE1 = NULL,   TE2 = NULL,   TE3 = NULL,   TM = NULL,   append_basis = NULL,   remove_basis = NULL,   remove_external_basis = NULL,   pre_align = TRUE,   pre_align_max_shift = 40,   pre_align_ref_freq = c(2.01, 3.03, 3.22),   pre_align_ref_amp = 1,   dfp_corr = TRUE,   dfp_corr_ref_subset = NULL,   output_ratio = NULL,   ecc = NULL,   hsvd_width = NULL,   decimate = FALSE,   trunc_fid_pts = NULL,   fit_method = NULL,   fit_opts = NULL,   fit_subset = NULL,   w_ref_subset = NULL,   legacy_ws = FALSE,   w_att = 0.7,   w_conc = 35880,   use_basis_cache = "auto",   summary_measures = NULL,   dyn_av_block_size = NULL,   dyn_av_scheme = NULL,   dyn_av_scheme_file = NULL,   dyn_basis_lb = NULL,   dyn_basis_lg = NULL,   lcm_bin_path = NULL,   plot_ppm_xlim = NULL,   extra_output = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE,   return_fit = FALSE,   write_preproc_metab_path = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Standard SVS 1H brain analysis pipeline. — fit_svs
text: input path mrs_data object containing MRS data. w_ref path mrs_data object containing MRS water reference data. output_dir directory path output fitting results. mri filepath nifti object containing anatomical MRI data. mri_seg filepath nifti object containing segmented MRI data. deface option apply faceoff mri input. Defaults FALSE. segment_t1 segment t1 weighted mri file ANTs use results perform partial volume correction. Defaults FALSE. segment_t1_method one : "rpyants" (default), "ants" "fslr". external_basis precompiled basis set object use analysis. append_external_basis append external basis internally generated one. Useful adding experimentally acquired baseline signals internally simulated basis sets. Defaults FALSE - meaning signals external basis used analysis. p_vols numeric vector partial volumes expressed percentages. Defaults 100% white matter. voxel containing 100% gray matter tissue use : p_vols = c(WM = 0, GM = 100, CSF = 0). format Override automatic data format detection. See format argument read_mrs() permitted values. pul_seq Pulse sequence use basis simulation. Can one following values : "press", "press_ideal", "press_shaped", "steam" "slaser". "press" "press_ideal" assumed unless magnetic field stronger 2.8 Tesla, "press_shaped" assumed 2.9 Tesla . TE metabolite mrs data echo time seconds. supplied guessed metab data file. TR metabolite mrs data repetition time seconds. supplied guessed metab data file. TE1 PRESS sLASER sequence timing parameter seconds. TE2 PRESS sLASER sequence timing parameter seconds. TE3 sLASER sequence timing parameter seconds. TM STEAM mixing time parameter seconds. append_basis names extra signals add default basis. Eg append_basis = c("peth", "cit"). Use get_mol_names() function print available signals. used precompiled basis sets. remove_basis grep expression match names signals remove basis. example: use "^lac$|^ala$" remove lactate alanine; "*" remove signals "^mm|^lip" remove macromolecular lipid signals. operation performed signals added append_basis. used precompiled/exernal basis sets. remove_external_basis grep expression match names signals remove external basis. example: use "^Lac$|^Ala$" remove lactateand alanine "^MM|^Lip" remove macromolecular lipid signals. pre_align perform simple frequency alignment known reference peaks. pre_align_max_shift maximum allowable shift Hz. Defaults 40 Hz. pre_align_ref_freq reference frequency ppm units. one frequency may specified. Defaults : c(2.01, 3.03, 3.22). pre_align_ref_amp reference frequency relative amplitudes. Defaults 1, corresponding equal amplitudes. dfp_corr perform dynamic frequency phase correction using RATS method. dfp_corr_ref_subset specify subset dynamics use reference scans dynamic frequency phase correction. example, 1:16 use mean first 16 dynamic scans reference spectrum. output_ratio optional string specify metabolite ratio output. Defaults "tCr". Multiple metabolites may specified multiple outputs. Set NA omit. ecc option perform water reference based eddy current correction, default apply unless GE format. hsvd_width set width HSVD filter Hz. Note applied width -width +width Hz, 0 Hz defined centre spectral width. Default disabled (set NULL), 30 Hz reasonable value. decimate option decimate data factor 2 analysis. Defaults FALSE. trunc_fid_pts number points truncate input data time-domain. E.g. setting 1024 ensure data time-domain points truncated length 1024. Defaults NULL, truncation performed. fit_method can "ABFIT-REG" "LCMODEL. Defaults "ABFIT-REG". fit_opts options pass fitting method. fit_subset specify subset dynamics analyse, example 1:16 fit first 16 dynamic scans. w_ref_subset specify subset dynamics use water reference following rats alignment averaging. example c(1, 2) use average first two dynamic water reference scans. Default value NULL uses available water reference scans. legacy_ws perform output legacy water scaling compatible default LCModel TARQUIN behaviour. See w_att w_conc arguments change default assumptions. Default value FALSE. w_att water attenuation factor (default = 0.7) legacy water scaling. Assumes water T2 80ms TE = 30 ms. exp(-30ms / 80ms) ~ 0.7. w_conc assumed water concentration (default = 35880) legacy water scaling. Default value corresponds typical white matter. Set 43300 gray matter, 55556 phantom measurements. use_basis_cache Pre-cache basis sets reduce analysis speed. Can one following : "auto", "" "none". default value "auto" use cache 3T PRESS - generally requires detailed simulation due high CSD. summary_measures output additional table subset metabolite levels, eg c("tNAA", "tNAA/tCr", "tNAA/tCho", "Lac/tNAA"). dyn_av_block_size perform temporal averaging specified block size. Defaults NULL, eg average across dynamic scans. dyn_av_scheme numeric vector sequential integers (starting 1), length number dynamic scans metabolite data. example: c(1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 3, 1, 1). dyn_av_scheme_file file path containing single column sequential integers (starting 1) length number dynamic scans metabolite data. File may formatted .xlsx, .xls, text csv format. dyn_basis_lb dynamic basis line-broadening apply Hz. dyn_basis_lg dynamic basis Lorentz-Gauss lineshape factor 0 1. Defaults 0, pure Lorentzian. lcm_bin_path set path LCModel binary. plot_ppm_xlim plotting ppm axis limits html results. results. extra_output write extra output files generating custom plots. Defaults FALSE. verbose output potentially useful information. return_fit return fit object, defaults FALSE. write_preproc_metab_path path write preprocessed metabolite data NIfTI format. overwrite overwrite existing fitting result files, defaults FALSE.

title: Standard SVS 1H brain analysis pipeline. — fit_svs
code: metab <- system.file("extdata", "philips_spar_sdat_WS.SDAT",                      package = "spant") w_ref <- system.file("extdata", "philips_spar_sdat_W.SDAT",                      package = "spant") out_dir <- file.path("~", "fit_svs_result") if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit_result <- fit_svs(metab, w_ref, out_dir) } # }

Use get_mol_names() function print available signals. used precompiled basis sets. remove_basis_ed_off grep expression match names signals remove basis. example: use "lac|ala" remove lactate alanine; "*" remove signals "^mm|^lip" remove macromolecular lipid signals. operation performed signals added append_basis_ed_off. used precompiled basis sets. pre_align perform simple frequency alignment known reference peaks. dfp_corr perform dynamic frequency phase correction using RATS method. output_ratio optional string specify metabolite ratio output. Defaults "tCr". Multiple metabolites may specified multiple outputs. Set NA omit. ecc option perform water reference based eddy current correction, default apply unless GE format. hsvd_width set width HSVD filter Hz. Note applied width -width +width Hz, 0 Hz defined centre spectral width. Default disabled (set NULL), 30 Hz reasonable value. decimate option decimate data factor 2 analysis. Defaults FALSE. trunc_fid_pts number points truncate input data time-domain. E.g. setting 1024 ensure data time-domain points truncated length 1024. Defaults NULL, truncation performed. fit_opts_edited options pass fitting method edited spectrum. fit_opts_ed_off options pass fitting method edit-spectrum. fit_subset specify subset dynamics analyse, example 1:16 fit first 16 dynamic scans. legacy_ws perform output legacy water scaling compatible default LCModel TARQUIN behaviour. See w_att w_conc arguments change default assumptions. Default value FALSE. w_att water attenuation factor (default = 0.7) legacy water scaling. Assumes water T2 80ms TE = 30 ms. exp(-30ms / 80ms) ~ 0.7. w_conc assumed water concentration (default = 35880) legacy water scaling. Default value corresponds typical white matter. Set 43300 gray matter, 55556 phantom measurements. use_basis_cache Pre-cache basis sets reduce analysis speed. Can one following : "auto", "" "none". default value "auto" use cache 3T PRESS - generally requires detailed simulation due high CSD. summary_measures output additional table subset metabolite levels, eg c("tNAA", "tNAA/tCr", "tNAA/tCho", "Lac/tNAA"). dyn_av_block_size perform temporal averaging specified block size. Defaults NULL, eg average across dynamic scans. dyn_av_scheme numeric vector sequential integers (starting 1), length number dynamic scans metabolite data. example: c(1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 3, 1, 1). dyn_av_scheme_file file path containing single column sequential integers (starting 1) length number dynamic scans metabolite data. File may formatted .xlsx, .xls, text csv format. plot_ppm_xlim plotting ppm axis limits html results. results. extra_output write extra output files generating custom plots. Defaults FALSE. verbose output potentially useful information. return_fit return fit object, defaults FALSE. overwrite overwrite existing fitting result files, defaults FALSE.

code: rats(   mrs_data,   ref = NULL,   xlim = c(4, 0.5),   max_shift = 20,   p_deg = 2,   sp_N = 2,   sp_deg = 3,   max_t = 0.2,   basis_type = "poly",   rescale_output = TRUE,   phase_corr = TRUE,   ret_corr_only = TRUE,   zero_freq_shift_t0 = FALSE,   list_mean_ref = TRUE,   remove_freq_outliers = FALSE,   freq_outlier_thresh = 3,   remove_phase_outliers = FALSE,   phase_outlier_thresh = 3,   remove_amp_outliers = FALSE,   amp_outlier_thresh = 3 )

title: Robust Alignment to a Target Spectrum (RATS). — rats
text: mrs_data MRS data corrected. ref optional MRS data use reference, mean dynamics used argument supplied. xlim optional frequency range perform optimisation, set NULL use full range. max_shift maximum allowable frequency shift Hz. p_deg polynomial degree used baseline modelling. Negative values disable baseline modelling. sp_N number spline functions, note true number sp_N + sp_deg. sp_deg degree spline functions. max_t truncate FID longer max_t reduce time taken, set NULL use entire FID. basis_type may one "poly" "spline". rescale_output rescale bl_matched_spec bl output improve consistency dynamic scans. phase_corr apply phase correction (addition frequency). TRUE default. ret_corr_only return corrected mrs_data object . zero_freq_shift_t0 perform linear fit frequency shifts set (linearly modeled) shift 0 Hz first dynamic scan. list_mean_ref ref specified list provided, use list mean scan reference. Otherwise use mean list element reference. remove_freq_outliers remove dynamics based frequency shift. freq_outlier_thresh threshold remove frequency outliers. remove_phase_outliers remove dynamics based phase shift. phase_outlier_thresh threshold remove phase outliers. remove_amp_outliers remove dynamics based amplitude change. amp_outlier_thresh threshold remove amplitude outliers.

title: Robust Alignment to a Target Spectrum (RATS). — rats
text: list containing corrected data; phase shift values units degrees Hz respectively.

what: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal
title: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal
text: Note, function assumes volume contains homogeneous voxel, eg pure WM, GM  CSF. Also note case homogeneous voxel relative densities MR-visible water (eg GM=0.78, WM=0.65, CSF=0.97) cancel need considered. Use scale_amp_molal_pvc volumes containing  multiple compartments. Details method can found "Use tissue water concentration reference proton spectroscopic imaging" Gasparovic et al MRM 2006 55(6):1219-26.

title: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal
code: scale_amp_molal(   fit_result,   ref_data,   te,   tr,   water_t1,   water_t2,   metab_t1,   metab_t2,   ... )

title: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal
text: fit_result result object generated fitting. ref_data water reference MRS data object. te MRS TE seconds. tr MRS TR seconds. water_t1 assumed water T1 value. water_t2 assumed water T2 value. metab_t1 assumed metabolite T1 value. metab_t2 assumed metabolite T2 value. ... additional arguments get_td_amp function.

title: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal
text: fit_result object rescaled results table.

what: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal_pvc
title: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal_pvc
text: Details method can found "Use tissue water concentration reference proton spectroscopic imaging" Gasparovic et al MRM 2006 55(6):1219-26. 1.5 Tesla relaxation assumptions taken paper. 3 Tesla data, relaxation assumptions taken "NMR relaxation times human brain 3.0 Tesla" Wansapura et al J Magn Reson Imaging 1999 9(4):531-8.

title: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal_pvc
Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal_pvc
text: fit_result result object generated fitting. ref_data water reference MRS data object. p_vols numeric vector partial volumes expressed percentages. example, voxel containing 100% white matter tissue use : p_vols = c(WM = 100, GM = 0, CSF = 0). te MRS TE seconds. tr MRS TR seconds. ... additional arguments get_td_amp function.

title: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / kg of tissue water). — scale_amp_molal_pvc
text: fit_result object rescaled results table.

text: See LCModel manual (section 10.2) water-scaling details assumptions relevant references. Use type concentration scaling compare fit results LCModel TARQUIN defaults. Otherwise scale_amp_molal_pvc generally preferred method.

title: Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / Litre of tissue). This function is depreciated, please use scale_amp_legacy instead. — scale_amp_molar

Apply water reference scaling to a fitting results object to yield metabolite quantities in millimolar (mM) units (mol / Litre of tissue). This function is depreciated, please use scale_amp_legacy instead. — scale_amp_molar
text: fit_result result object generated fitting. ref_data water reference MRS data object. w_att water attenuation factor (default = 0.7). Assumes water T2 80ms TE = 30 ms. exp(-30ms / 80ms) ~ 0.7. w_conc assumed water concentration (default = 35880). Default value corresponds typical white matter. Set 43300 gray matter, 55556 phantom measurements. ... additional arguments get_td_amp function.

code: # S3 method for class 'fit_result' stackplot(   x,   xlim = NULL,   y_offset = 0,   dyn = 1,   x_pos = 1,   y_pos = 1,   z_pos = 1,   coil = 1,   n = NULL,   sub_bl = FALSE,   labels = FALSE,   label_names = NULL,   sig_col = "black",   restore_def_par = TRUE,   omit_signals = NULL,   combine_lipmm = FALSE,   combine_metab = FALSE,   mar = NULL,   show_grid = TRUE,   grid_nx = NULL,   grid_ny = NA,   invert_fit = FALSE,   ... )

title: Plot the fitting results of an object of class fit_result with individual basis set components shown. — stackplot.fit_result
text: x fit_result object. xlim range values display x-axis, eg xlim = c(4,1). y_offset separate basis signals y-axis direction value. dyn dynamic index plot. x_pos x index plot. y_pos y index plot. z_pos z index plot. coil coil element number plot. n single index element plot (overrides indices given). sub_bl subtract baseline data fit (logical). labels print signal labels right side plot. label_names provide character vector signal names replace defaults determined basis set. sig_col colour individual signal components. restore_def_par restore default plotting par values plot made. omit_signals character vector basis signal names removed plot. combine_lipmm combine basis signals names starting "Lip" "MM". combine_metab combine basis signals names starting "Lip" "MM". mar option adjust plot margins. See ?par. show_grid plot gridlines behind data (logical). Defaults TRUE. grid_nx number cells grid x y direction. NULL grid aligns tick marks corresponding default axis (.e., tickmarks computed axTicks). NA, grid lines drawn corresponding direction. grid_ny . invert_fit show fit result "upside-"/ ... arguments plot method.

code: # S3 method for class 'mrs_data' stackplot(   x,   xlim = NULL,   mode = "re",   x_units = NULL,   fd = TRUE,   col = NULL,   alpha = NULL,   x_offset = 0,   y_offset = 0,   plot_dim = NULL,   x_pos = NULL,   y_pos = NULL,   z_pos = NULL,   dyn = 1,   coil = 1,   bty = NULL,   labels = NULL,   lab_cex = 1,   bl_lty = NULL,   bl_lwd = 0.5,   restore_def_par = TRUE,   show_grid = NULL,   grid_nx = NULL,   grid_ny = NA,   lwd = NULL,   vline = NULL,   vline_lty = 2,   vline_col = "red",   mar = NULL,   y_scale = FALSE,   ... )

title: Stackplot plotting method for objects of class mrs_data. — stackplot.mrs_data
text: x object class mrs_data. xlim range values display x-axis, eg xlim = c(4,1). mode representation complex numbers plotted, can one : "re", "im", "mod" "arg". x_units units use x-axis, can one : "ppm", "hz", "points" "seconds". fd display data frequency-domain (default), time-domain (logical). col set colour line, eg col = rgb(1, 0, 0, 0.5). alpha set line transparency, eg alpha = 0.5 50% transparency. Overrides transparency levels set col. x_offset separate plots x-axis direction value. Default value 0. y_offset separate plots y-axis direction value. plot_dim dimension display y-axis, can one : "dyn", "x", "y", "z", "coil" NULL. NULL (default) spectra collapsed dynamic dimension displayed. x_pos x index plot. y_pos y index plot. z_pos z index plot. dyn dynamic index plot. coil coil element number plot. bty option draw box around plot. See ?par. labels add labels data item. lab_cex label size. bl_lty linetype y = 0 baseline trace. default value NULL results baseline plotted. bl_lwd linewith y = 0 baseline trace. Defaults 0.5. restore_def_par restore default plotting par values plot made. show_grid plot gridlines behind data (logical). Defaults TRUE. grid_nx number cells grid x y direction. NULL grid aligns tick marks corresponding default axis (.e., tickmarks computed axTicks). NA, grid lines drawn corresponding direction. grid_ny . lwd plot linewidth. vline x-value draw vertical line. vline_lty linetype vertical line. vline_col colour vertical line. mar option adjust plot margins. See ?par. y_scale option display y-axis values (logical). ... arguments pass matplot method.

Defaults 100% white matter. voxel containing 100% gray matter tissue use : p_vols = c(WM = 0, GM = 100, CSF = 0). append_basis names extra signals add default basis. Eg append_basis = c("peth", "cit"). used precompiled basis sets. remove_basis names signals remove basis. used precompiled basis sets. dfp_corr perform dynamic frequency phase correction using RATS method. omit_bad_dynamics detect remove bad dynamics. te metabolite mrs data echo time seconds. supplied guessed metab data file. tr metabolite mrs data repetition time seconds. supplied guessed metab data file. output_ratio optional string specify metabolite ratio output. Defaults "tCr" multiple metabolites may specified multiple outputs. Set NULL omit. ecc option perform water reference based eddy current correction, defaults FALSE. abfit_opts options pass ABfit. verbose output potentially useful information.

title: spant 4.3.0
text: Bug fix mr_data2bids function. Bug fix testing GE data fit_svs. Added fit_svs_btrg_v1 function simple wrapper fit_svs defaults adjusted improve analysis lower quality brain tumour spectra.

title: spant 4.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-20 Added rm_basis_elements function. Added plot_tqn_fit_csv function. Test fix NIfTI MRS reader.

title: spant 4.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-21 Update lsei.f fix warning produced GCC 16. Changed default segmentation method rpyants fit_svs.

title: spant 4.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-08 qr.solve reverts ginv rats rare cases qr.solve fails. fit_svs updated accept single argument remove_basis. Bug fix Siemens DICOM reader. Added support T1 tissue segmentation using binary installation ANTs : https://github.com/antsx/ants Added support T1 defacing FaceOff method : https://github.com/srikash/FaceOff Added cross-platform support T1 tissue segmentation using ANTs via rpyANTs package : https://github.com/dipterix/rpyANTs

title: spant 3.9.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-23 Added extra pre_align options fit_svs. Added extra plot fit_svs html output help diagnose frequency alignment problems.

title: spant 3.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-12-16 Added deface option fit_svs. Added overwrite option fit_svs. fit_svs fit_svs_edited now accept vectorised file inputs batch analyses.

title: spant 3.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-11-20 Added lb_renoise function fMRS analyses. Added paths2df function help extract ID fields vector paths. Added trim_paths match_files functions help filesystem based data discovery.

title: spant 3.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-22 Added mean_dyns_scheme function. Millisecond option added plot x scale. Added read_basis_niidir write_basis_niidir functions import/export basis sets stored group NIfTI MRS files.

title: spant 3.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-08-18 Added function help bids sorting MRS MRI data : mr_data2bids. Extra options fit_svs : dfp_corr_ref_subset w_ref_subset. fit_svs default now uses mean available water reference scans, rather just first dynamic. Set w_ref_subset = 1 reproduce previous results.

title: spant 3.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-06-11 Added function read DKD’s sLASER MoCo log file. Bug fix TE parameter determination Siemens IMA files.

title: spant 3.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-03-28 Added voxel render MRI images fit_svs. Added voxel segmentation render support fit_svs. Bug fix TE parameter determination Siemens TWIX files. Added extra output data fit_svs aid group analyses.

title: spant 3.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-02-14 Individual metabolite plots html report now labelled workaround R Markdown bug identical plots (eg signal zero) sometimes shown. Added get_hrf function spant namespace. Tentative support command line interface : spant_fit_svs. Tentative support LCModel analysis fit_svs function cli.

title: spant 3.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-17 Tentative implementation standard single voxel MRS processing pipeline function: fit_svs. spant user guide added project: https://spantdoc.wilsonlab.co.uk/ Implemented basis caching sim_basis. scale_amp_molar function depreciated. Please use scale_amp_legacy LCModel style concentration scaling instead. Assumed T1 T2 relaxation values molal concentration scaling updated. See user guide details.

title: spant 2.24.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-11-14 Added argument adjust legend font size ortho3 function. Improved metabolite simulation performance thanks expm package recently adding support complex matrices.

title: spant 2.23.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-08-27 abfit options lipid MM maximum broadening maximum frequency shift now default metabolite values unless explicitly changed. Added function calculate correlation matrix basis_set. Fix RDA files comma sometimes used decimal point.

title: spant 2.22.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-07-11 Added find_bids_mrs mrs_data2bids helper functions. Improved handling CMRR semi-LASER SVS water reference scans. Added functions GLS coil combination.

title: spant 2.21.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-05-30 Fixed compiler warnings Fortran code. Added glm_spec function. Added add_noise_spec_snr function.

title: spant 2.20.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-05-01 Added vertical line colour linetype options stackplots. Added option give Y axis labels units seconds spectrograms.

title: spant 2.19.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-03-22 Removed dependency smoother package. Added functions generating BOLD trapezoidal response shapes.

title: spant 2.18.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-02-23 Improved automated file type detection. Bug fixes DICOM MRS reader. scale_spec function now works basis set objects. Added invert_fit option plotting methods.

title: spant 2.17.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-11-28 Simulation duration output now rounded 2dp. sim_basis now accepts character vector molecular names. Added get_1h_brain_basis_names function.

title: spant 2.16.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-10-23 Added subset option mean_dyns function. Added vline option plot. Added common 31P brain metabolites. Added verbose option sim_basis.

title: spant 2.15.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-09-14 SNR estimate zero comb_coils now failure case. Bug fix GE pfile reader header revision 20. Added function finding valid MRS files : find_mrs_files.

title: spant 2.14.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-07-19 Added vline argument stackplot function. Added functions set MRS data TR number transients.

title: spant 2.13.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-06-26 Improved defaults sim_brain_1h function. Added scale_basis_amp function. Added crop_basis function.

title: spant 2.12.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-05-22 Amplitude scaling functions duplicate water reference amplitudes sensible. Added hline option mrs_data plot function.

title: spant 2.11.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-04-22 Bug fix Siemens RDA slice thickness detection. ProtocolName now read GE p-files.

title: spant 2.10.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-03-20 Tentative support UIH DICOM MRS format. Improved support reading GE p-files. Added WIP function export one-page summary pdf fit result “one_page_pdf”.

title: spant 2.9.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-03-03 Bug fix using align function masked data. mean_dyn_blocks returns input data block_size set 1.

title: spant 2.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-01-28 Added Manufacturer tag metadata. Added mask_xy_corners mask_xy_ellipse functions. Added functions fit T1 T2 relaxation curves. Updated CI checks.

title: spant 2.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-01-06 Masked spectra plotted image now shown value zero. Added function read .pta files - read_pulse_pta. Added function make basis set objects directory LCModel formatted RAW files - make_basis_from_raw. Added horizontal line fit result plots. Improvements reading LCM .raw files variable numerical columns.

title: spant 2.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-12-06 Improved function documentation water concentration scaling. Added water tCr scaling examples introduction vignette. Better error checking reading ima files. read_basis function improved work variable formatting. Improved default method time-domain signal amplitude estimation. Added helper functions working basis set objects: scale_basis_from_singlet, resample_basis get_basis_subset. Bug fix STEAM basis simulation incorrectly scaled internal water scaling methods.

title: spant 2.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-11-07 Performance improvement reading VD format TWIX files. Function added scale metabolite levels set value (scale_amp_ratio_value).

title: spant 2.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-10-12 Verbose option added read_ima_coil_dir read_ima_dyn_dir functions. Support added reading jMRUI txt formatted MRS data. sim_brain_1h default sequence changed semi-LASER line-broadening option added. Bug fix reading LCModel RAW files. Bug fix HSVD filter 0 1 components found filter region. Improved removal unwanted data points reading TWIX formatted data. json sidecar automatically written exporting NIfTI MRS formatted data. TWIX reader improved read data points last scan file. also fixes rare bug.

title: spant 2.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-09-08 peak_info function now works masked datasets. Bug fix HSVD function working frequency domain data. Bug fix TWIX affine calculation using power two matrix dimensions. New function (crop_td_pts_pot) round dataset length nearest power 2. Added ret_metab_only option comb_coils. rats now returns corrected data default, note break old code.

title: spant 2.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-07-21 y-axis label can now changed new argument (yaxis_lab) plot.mrs_data. Segmentation files now checked non-integer values. gridplot better row col number defaults MRSI, bug fix plotting masked spectra. Updated functions dealing lists MRSI datasets. Bug fix crop_xy function working odd matrix sizes.

title: spant 2.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-06-22 magrittr pipe (%>%) longer loaded together spant now base pipe part R. Vignettes tests updated use base pipe. NIfTI MRS writer longer fails dealing NULL affine matrix. Bug fix NIfTI MRS writer, frequency domain data correctly converted time-domain export.

title: spant 1.22.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-27 Added MSM acetone set internally defined molecules. write_mrs_nifti function removed userspace. write_mrs used instead. set_td_pts crop_td_pts now work lists spectra. Multiple files can now written passing list spectra write_mrs.

title: spant 1.21.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-03 Bug fix get_mrsi_voi function due incorrect rounding MRSI matrix dimensions cases. Added mask_fit_res function. Option added get_2d_psf function ensure odd output dimensions. Bug fix using rats datasets masked voxels. Added spec_decomp function. Added ret_scale_factor option scale_spec function.

title: spant 1.20.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-03-30 Options added ft_dyns return modulus 2D spectra pre-transform chemical shift axis needed. Bug fix printing lists mrs_data objects. comb_coils function now supports lists mrs_data objects. IMA reader now respects verbose option read_mrs. Bug fix get_mrsi_voi ignoring ker argument. Bug fix reading Siemens TWIX files.

title: spant 1.19.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-02-14 Added sum_mrs_list function. Bug fix operating mrs_data single values aren’t numeric types. example, multiplying mrs_data single complex number now works. number common preprocessing functions now operate lists mrs_data objects. Change NIfTI MRS reader fix failing test using development version RNifti.

title: spant 1.18.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-02-08 Better behaviour using stackplot single spectra datasets. Better behaviour using image single spectra datasets. Bug fix reading MEGA-PRESS data TWIX format. Better error checking lists spectra. Bug fix calculating NAA linewidth MEGA-PRESS data.

title: spant 1.17.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-01-07 New function added (recon_twix_2d) reconstructing basic phase encoded twix 2D MRSI data. File name argument read_mrs now permits globbing, eg read_mrs(“*.dcm”). Improved plotting metabolite maps containing infinite values. Improvements GE p-file reader. Bug fix TWIX MRSI voxel dimensions. get_mrsi2d_seg now returns partial volume maps well data frame. Improved error handling spec_op function. apodise_xy can now applied multi-coil dynamic datasets.

title: spant 1.16.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-11-18 package published Journal Open Source Software : “spant: R package magnetic resonance spectroscopy analysis. JOSS 2021, 6(67), 3646”. basis result HSVD function now mrs_data object. complete model signal now output HSVD function. image function x-axis updated consistent plotting methods. Minor refactor simulation code dependency swap complexplus exmp packages.

title: spant 1.15.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-10-22 Added unit test reading writing LCM .basis formatted files. Added FWHM estimates tCr tCho ABfit. Added water suppression efficiency water FWHM measures diagnostic output svs_1h_brain_analysis. Added get_head_dyns get_tail_dyns return first last dynamic scans within dataset. Fixed CI errors. Improved installation instructions. Removed comb_csv_results function reduced number required packages.

title: spant 1.14.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-07-23 Added glycerol simulation parameters, e.g. get_mol_paras(“glyc”). Bug fix read_ima_* functions. Improved y = 0 baseline stackplot setting bl_lty parameter. Removed norm_mrs function replaced scale_spec simple data scaling tasks, eg scaling based integration spectral region. Added spec_op function performing simple summary operations spectral segments. Changed name scale_mrs function scale_mrs_amp. Added mean_mrs_list function. Improved LCM RAW BASIS readers (contribution Alex Craven).

title: spant 1.13.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-06-09 ABfit frequency shifts limits now specified ppm rather Hz improve consistency field strengths. NAA linewidth now estimated output ABfit NAAG absent - useful BRAINO phantom scans. Warning now given spectra mathematically combined time/frequency domain. sum_mrs function added simplify combining spectra within pipe. scale_mrs function added simplify scaling spectrum within pipe. add_noise function added simplify generating simulated data pipe. hsvd_filt function now accepts frequency range ppm units gives option return model rather filtered data. Bug fix hsvd_filt function max_damp argument ignored. SVS reference scans, found TWIX files, now removed default. Bug fix reading list/data one coil element used.

title: spant 1.12.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-05-05 Added 2HG citrate simulation parameters. e.g. get_mol_paras(“2hg”). Better print output molecular definitions. Added metabolite basis simulation vignettes. Bug fix setting ppm reference reading LCModel RAW files. Bug fix ABfit CRLB calculation combined signals, eg tNAA, tCr.

title: spant 1.11.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-03-28 Options added allow extra information attached mrs_data fit_result objects data frame. New functions (combine_fit_XXX) working multiple fit results contained within list structure. Basis set noise region now checked validity ABfit. Added spant_abfit_benchmark function. Tentative functions performing “standard” 1H brain analyses: svs_1h_brain_analysis svs_1h_brain_batch_analysis. Improved support LCModel analyses.

title: spant 1.10.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-01-06 Fix NIfTI MRS reader/writer. ortho3 now shows correct labels orientations RAS. ortho3_int function renamed ortho3_inter. Argument order change plot_voi_overlay plot_voi_overlay_seg consistent ortho3. Regression fix partial volume segmentation plotting. Echo time parameter now stored meta structure. ABfit now performs 1D phase parameter search prefit stage improve reliability. May disabled prefit_phase_search fit option.

title: spant 1.9.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-12-15 NIfTI MRS reader writer now uses header extension metadata. Thanks Jon Clayden adding extension read/write support RNifti package. Default plots now gridlines y-direction plot line now thicker coloured blue. Opacity option added plotting functions (alpha). Bug fix comb_coils SVS data. Bug fixes Siemens geometry information. Changed ordering arguments write_mrs write_mrs_nifti improve consistency functions. Internal function (ortho3) now used plot voxel locations MRI. Internal dicom reader function added. Tentative support Siemens Philips DICOM MRS format added. Internal changes way orientation information handled.

title: spant 1.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-11-18 Added gridplot function. New functions added sampling. Added signal space projection method MRSI. Geometry information now read Siemens twix files. GitHub actions now used continuous integration instead Travis AppVeyor. Added precomp function avoid repeated computation. mrs_data2mat function now collapses dimensions dynamics default. mrs_data objects now store nucleus. Added reader old Varian format (fid/procpar) data.

title: spant 1.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-07-09 Added write_mrs function guesses output format file extension can specified argument. write_mrs_XXX functions depreciated. read_mrs function now tries guess format file extension. Added json sidecar NIFTI MRS export function. Added option read MRS data NIFTI file json sidecar using read_mrs function. Changed default crop_spec region 4.0 0.2 ppm.

title: spant 1.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-06-05 Bug fix GE P file reader. Added downsample_mrs function. Tentative function (write_mrs_nifti) write MRS data NIFTI format file evaluation purposes .

title: spant 1.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-05-11 Added option ABfit allow metabolite amplitudes negative (ahat_calc_method). Removed lsei package dependency.

title: spant 1.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-04-20 Added option plot y = 0 baseline trace stackplot.mrs_data. Added convenience functions read write mrs_data rds format.

title: spant 1.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-04-03 Added get_fit_table function combine fit tables fit_result object single dataframe. ppm function can now applied fit result objects. Bug fix plot_slice_fit_inter added option specify denominator. Added option specify denominator plot_slice_fit.

title: spant 1.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2020-03-15 Added option display progress bar fit_mrs function better conformance “Writing R Extensions” non-interactive use. Changed test tolerance accommodate differences OSX.

title: spant 1.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-03-11 Performance improvement HSVD water filter. ABfit unit tests now run simulated data improve consistency different platforms. Improvements fit amplitude scaling code. Improved checking mrs_data processing functions. Added preprocessing steps vignette.

title: spant 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-03-01 Improved ppm labels ABfit plot results. Bug fix plot_slice_fit using fits masked data. Updated unit tests. Added vignette manually adjusting ABfit baseline smoothness. Updated introduction vignette. Bug fixes image stackplot functions masked MRS data. Bug fix using RATS masked MRS data. Changes prepare R 4.0.0.

title: spant 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-02-17 ABfit analysis method added, now default mrs_fit function. Added reader LCModel RAW format data. Added read_mri_dyn_dir function reading dynamic MRS exported Siemens scanners. Bug fixes 2D MRSI voxel segmentation calculation. Bug fixes Siemens IMA format reader SVS data. Optional colourbar added ortho3 function.

title: spant 0.19.0
text: CRAN release: 2019-12-06 Added Asc, BHB, Cho, PEth Ser simulation parameters. Added ker option get_mrsi_voi function. Added append_basis function combine two basis sets objects. align function now accepts one reference frequency.

title: spant 0.17.0
text: Added function grid shift 2D MRSI data x/y direction. Better plotting/fitting support masking data setting data points NA. Bug fix interactive voxel selection position indicator. Added mask_xy mask voxels centred rectangular region. Minor changes improve parallel processing support.

title: spant 0.16.0
text: CRAN release: 2019-10-30 SNR now defined max data point divided standard deviation noise (n.b. factor two removed -line upcoming terminology paper). Default rats method improved work multidimensional datasets. Added norm_mrs function normalise intensity spectral data. Added bc_constant function correct spectral baselines constant offset. Added re_weighting function apply resolution enhancement weighting FID. Performance improvement apodise_xy function. sd function now works mrs_data. Added 2D MRSI support Siemens IMA format.

title: spant 0.15.0
text: CRAN release: 2019-09-19 Bug fix using auto_phase function single spectrum. Bug fix comb_coils returning unaveraged data requested. Added options combine metabolite signals stackplot fit object adjust plot margins. Added comb_fits function. Added collapse_to_dyns function mrs_fit objects. RDA reader now extracts geometry information.

title: spant 0.14.0
text: CRAN release: 2019-05-28 Added options omit basis signals, change label names combine lipid MM signals stackplot fit object. Added auto_phase function zeroth order phase-correction simple spectra. Added get_subset function aid MRSI slicing. Added decimate_mrs function. Added fit_amps function quickly extract amplitude estimates fit object. Bug fix int_spec function. sim_basis function arguments updated accept acq_par objects.

title: spant 0.13.0
text: CRAN release: 2019-04-25 Various bug fixes Siemens TWIX reader. rats tdsr functions now use mean spectrum default reference. Added option remove x axis mrs_data plot. Added ylim y_scale options fit plotting. Added %$% operator magrittr package. Added interpolation option calc_spec_snr. Added hline vline options image.mrs_data.

title: spant 0.12.0
text: CRAN release: 2018-11-05 Fit results stackplot now option display labels. Added option reverse eddy current correction. Improved GE p-file reader. diff function can now applied mrs_data objects. Complex functions: Re, Im, Mod, Arg Conj can now applied mrs_data objects. Default simulations Glc NAAG improved.

title: spant 0.11.0
text: CRAN release: 2018-07-20 Added mar argument plot command. td2fd fd2td now give warnings used data already target domain. Improved documentation formatting consistency fixed spelling errors. Added rats method.

title: spant 0.10.0
text: CRAN release: 2018-06-25 names -built pulse sequence functions now start seq_* make easier find. Added new functions simulate following MRS sequences: CPMG, MEGA-PRESS, STEAM, sLASER. sLASER sequence kindly contributed Pierre-Gilles Henry. Bug fix get_mol_names function. stackplot function now accepts labels argument time-domain plotting. def_acq_paras function now accepts arguments override defaults. Added source field mol.paras object cite origin values. Option restore plotting par defaults. magrittr pipe operator now exported.

title: spant 0.9.0
text: CRAN release: 2018-03-07 Updated plotting modes one : “re”, “im”, “mod” “arg”. Updated int_spec function use “re”, “im”, “mod”. Added function replicate data across particular dimension. Added convenience function simulate normal looking 1H brain MRS data. phase shift functions now accept vector inputs.

title: spant 0.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2018-01-31 Added new function frequency drift correction. Added support Siemens ima TWIX SVS data. Added support GE p-file SVS data. Added apply_axes fn. Support reading SPM style segmentation results (spm_pve2categorical).

title: spant 0.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2017-10-25 Interactive plotting function added fit results - plot_fit_slice_inter. Bug fix appending dynamic results. Bug fix reading list data files without reference data. Bug fix append_dyns function. basis2mrs_data function extended allow summation basis elements setting individual amplitudes. Added shift function manual frequency shift adjustment. Added initial unit tests automated coveralls checking.

title: spant 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2017-10-10 default brain PRESS basis now simulated fit_mrs function basis argument isn’t specified. Added calc_peak_info function simple singlet analyses. crop_spec function now maintains original frequency scale. basis set used analyses now added fit result object. Bug fix simulating basis sets one element. lb function can now used basis-set objects. Bug fix spar_sdat reader non-localised MRS. AppVeyor now used test Windows compatibility - John Muschelli.

title: spant 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2017-08-09 Bug fix SPAR/SDAT SVS voxel dimensions. MRSI support added Philips SPAR/SDAT data. Fit plots now default full spectral range unless xlim specified. Fit plots allow x, y, z, coil, dynamic indices specified. Added option subtract baseline fit plots.

title: spant 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2017-06-20 Added stackplot method fit objects. Added functions registering visualising SVS volumes images performing partial volume correction. Philips “list data” also now reads noise scans. calc_coil_noise_cor, calc_coil_noise_sd functions added aid coil combination. Documentation updates plotting methods. Added simulation methods userland.

title: spant 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2017-05-24 Added Siemens RDA format reader. Added Philips “list data” format reader. Added Bruker paravision format reader. Added PROPACK option HSVD based filtering. Added coil combination function. Bug fix incorrect ppm scale fit plots fs != 2000Hz. Bug fix VARPRO analytical jacobian calculation.

title: spant 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2017-04-26 First public release.
